sacco

Class Wanderer

  • java.lang.Object
    • sacco.Wanderer
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Wanderer>

    public class Wanderer
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<Wanderer>
    A Wanderer is an object that walks around on the DrawingBoard. There are eight states, represented by the eight static ints RUN_UP, RUN_DOWN, RUN_LEFT, RUN_RIGHT, STAND_UP, STAND_DOWN, STAND_LEFT, and STAND_RIGHT.
    Author:
    13698

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      static int RUN_DOWN 
      static int RUN_LEFT 
      static int RUN_RIGHT 
      static int RUN_UP 
      static int STAND_DOWN 
      static int STAND_LEFT 
      static int STAND_RIGHT 
      static int STAND_UP 

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Wanderer(double x, double y)
      Constructs a Wanderer with a given x an y.
      Wanderer(Picture spriteSheet) 
      Wanderer(Picture spriteSheet, int spriteWidth, int spriteHeight) 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void changeState()
      Randomizes the state of the Wanderer.
      int compareTo(Wanderer w) 
      int getCharacterNumber()
      Returns the id number that represents the current character being displayed in the animation.
      int getHeight()
      Returns the height of the Wanderer
      double getSpeed()
      Returns the current speed of the Wanderer.
      int getState()
      Returns the current state of the Wanderer: The value will match one of the static ints RUN_UP=0, RUN_DOWN=1, RUN_LEFT=2, RUN_RIGHT=3, STAND_UP=4, STAND_DOWN=5, STAND_LEFT=6, STAND_RIGHT=7
      int getWidth()
      Returns the width of the Wanderer
      WanderWorld getWorld() 
      double getX()
      Returns the x coordinate of the leftmost edge of the Wanderer
      double getY()
      Returns the y coordinate of the topmost edge of the Wanderer
      boolean isActive()
      Returns true if this Wanderer is currently active
      boolean isFacingDown()
      Determines if the Wanderer is facing down
      boolean isFacingLeft()
      Determines if the Wanderer is facing left
      boolean isFacingRight()
      Determines if the Wanderer is facing right
      boolean isFacingUp()
      Determines if the Wanderer is facing up
      boolean isFacingUP()
      Only here so that the assignments before 2017-18 still function with the UP typo
      boolean isRunning()
      Returns true if the Wanderer is running in any direction
      boolean isStanding()
      Returns true if the Wanderer is standing, facing any direction
      void move()
      Moves the Wanderer in whichever direction its state dictates
      void paintSelf(Canvas brush)
      Paints the Wanderer
      void setAutomation(boolean b)
      Allows you to turn on or off the automatic decision making system.
      void setCharacterNumber(int idNum)
      Sets the character being displayed to the given ID number Precondition: The Character ID number is valid
      void setScale(double scale) 
      void setSpeed(double speed)
      Sets the current speed of the Wanderer.
      void setState(int state)
      Sets the current state of the Wanderer: Precondition: state matches one of the static state variables RUN_UP=0, RUN_DOWN=1, RUN_LEFT=2, RUN_RIGHT=3, STAND_UP=4, STAND_DOWN=5, STAND_LEFT=6, STAND_RIGHT=7
      void setWorld(WanderWorld myWorld) 
      void setX(double x)
      Sets the x coordinate to the given parameter
      void setXY(double x, double y)
      Sets both the x and the y coordinate
      void setY(double y)
      Sets the y coordinate to the given parameter
      void update()
      Updates the image for animation, and calls the move method.
      void updateImage()
      Moves the animation one frame

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Wanderer

        public Wanderer(double x,
                double y)
        Constructs a Wanderer with a given x an y. Character state is set randomly.
        Parameters:
        x - x
        y - y

      • Wanderer

        public Wanderer(Picture spriteSheet)

      • Wanderer

                int spriteWidth,
                int spriteHeight)

    • Method Detail

      • paintSelf

        public void paintSelf(Canvas brush)
        Paints the Wanderer
        Parameters:
        brush -

      • update

        public void update()
        Updates the image for animation, and calls the move method. There is a chance that a state change will happen if automation is turned on.

      • getWidth

        public int getWidth()
        Returns the width of the Wanderer
        Returns:
        the width

      • getHeight

        public int getHeight()
        Returns the height of the Wanderer
        Returns:

        Determines if the Wanderer is facing left
        Returns:

      • isFacingRight

        public boolean isFacingRight()
        Determines if the Wanderer is facing right
        Returns:

      • isFacingUP

        public boolean isFacingUP()
        Only here so that the assignments before 2017-18 still function with the UP typo
        Returns:

      • isFacingUp

        public boolean isFacingUp()
        Determines if the Wanderer is facing up
        Returns:

      • isFacingDown

        public boolean isFacingDown()
        Determines if the Wanderer is facing down
        Returns:

      • isRunning

        public boolean isRunning()
        Returns true if the Wanderer is running in any direction
        Returns:

      • isStanding

        public boolean isStanding()
        Returns true if the Wanderer is standing, facing any direction
        Returns:

      • move

        public void move()
        Moves the Wanderer in whichever direction its state dictates

      • updateImage

        public void updateImage()
        Moves the animation one frame

      • setAutomation

        public void setAutomation(boolean b)
        Allows you to turn on or off the automatic decision making system. When off, the Wanderer will remain in its current state unless manually changed
        Parameters:
        b -

      • getState

        public int getState()
        Returns the current state of the Wanderer: The value will match one of the static ints RUN_UP=0, RUN_DOWN=1, RUN_LEFT=2, RUN_RIGHT=3, STAND_UP=4, STAND_DOWN=5, STAND_LEFT=6, STAND_RIGHT=7
        Returns:
        The int that represents the current state of the Wanderer

      • setState

        public void setState(int state)
        Sets the current state of the Wanderer: Precondition: state matches one of the static state variables RUN_UP=0, RUN_DOWN=1, RUN_LEFT=2, RUN_RIGHT=3, STAND_UP=4, STAND_DOWN=5, STAND_LEFT=6, STAND_RIGHT=7
        Parameters:
        state - The int that represents the current state of the Wanderer

      • setSpeed

        public void setSpeed(double speed)
        Sets the current speed of the Wanderer. The default is 5.
        Parameters:
        speed -

      • getSpeed

        public double getSpeed()
        Returns the current speed of the Wanderer.
        Returns:

        Returns the x coordinate of the leftmost edge of the Wanderer
